name: Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC) API
what_it_does: Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC) REST API via Autodesk Platform Services (APS) for construction project management and BIM collaboration platform. Enables AI agents to manage construction RFI (Request for Information) creation and tracking for project communication, handle submittal review workflow automation for construction approvals, access BIM model data and design coordination clash detection results, retrieve construction document and drawing version management data, manage daily log and field report collection from construction sites, handle cost management and budget tracking data for construction finance, access schedule and milestone tracking for project timeline management, retrieve quality and safety checklist completion data for compliance, manage punch list and deficiency tracking for project closeout, and integrate construction data with BIM 360, Revit, and project management platforms.
use_cases: Tracking RFIs and submittals from construction coordination agents
Monitoring BIM clash detection results from design coordination agents
Aggregating field reports from construction site intelligence agents
Managing cost and schedule data from construction project management agents
not_for: Architecture design without construction project management and field coordination
Simple project management without BIM and construction document management
Manufacturing or non-AEC industries without construction workflow needs
best_when: A general contractor, owner, or construction manager using Autodesk Construction Cloud wants AI agents to automate RFI tracking, submittal review, BIM coordination, field reporting, and construction project analytics.
avoid_when: CONTRACTUAL RISK: Construction submittals, RFIs, and design coordination changes have contractual implications — automated approval or rejection of submittals without licensed professional review creates design liability. Automated schedule updates without project manager review can create false contractual milestones. BIM clash resolutions require engineering judgment that AI cannot provide without professional oversight.

auth.notes: Autodesk APS uses OAuth 2.0 with 3-legged (user) and 2-legged (service) flows. Comprehensive scopes per API surface (data:read, data:write, account:read, etc.). Open-source SDKs on GitHub (autodesk-platform-services). Webhooks for model and document events. Developer portal at aps.autodesk.com. San Francisco, California HQ.

pricing.free_tier_limits: APS developer plan with usage quotas; ACC subscription required for project data access
pricing.paid_tiers: APS tokens for compute; ACC subscription per user and project for data access
pricing.notes: San Francisco, California. Founded 1982. Public (ADSK NASDAQ). $5B+ annual revenue. AEC software market leader. Construction Cloud rebranded from BIM 360 (2020). 250,000+ construction firms. Strong in large GC and owner-contractor workflows. Open API ecosystem via APS (formerly Forge). Competes with Procore and Trimble for construction management market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per APS app; credits-based model for compute; documented per API

known_agent_gotchas: CONTRACTUAL RISK: Construction submittals and RFI responses have contractual and design liability implications; never auto-approve or auto-reject without licensed professional review
3-legged vs. 2-legged OAuth — user-specific operations (RFI approval, submittal review) require 3-legged OAuth with user consent; service-account operations can use 2-legged
BIM 360 to ACC migration — some customers still on BIM 360 API; ACC and BIM 360 have separate API endpoints; verify which platform the project is on before integration
Model derivative processing — BIM model file processing (viewable generation) is asynchronous with significant latency; poll job status rather than assuming immediate availability
ACC Hub and project scoping — ACC has Hub > Project > Folder hierarchy; all API calls require correct hub and project scoping; incorrect scoping causes silent empty results
Token credit model for APS — Autodesk APS uses a token credit model for compute operations (model viewing, translation); budget compute credits carefully for high-volume use
agent_readiness.security_notes: Construction management and BIM. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, FedRAMP. OAuth2 with granular scopes. US/EU/APAC. Construction project, BIM model, and field data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Construction and BIM platform. SOC2, FedRAMP, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Construction project and design data.
